book_source: Real corpus B (Salesforce) account NAMES fetched live (UCSM-P3E seed corpus); CS-platform/telemetry evidence is synthetic (no live CS-platform/telemetry connector exists in this architecture, matching Program 4's cross-system-beat precedent). Real Salesforce numeric ids are never persisted -- opaque ids are the join key.

output.customer_draft:
Hi Ops Lead,

We're seeing an adoption gap on your FleetOps Platform rollout: seat penetration is still low, which limits how much of the purchased workflow your team can put into day-to-day use. With an activation step dated for July 10, I'd like to check whether this gap is putting your timeline at risk before it slips.

Could we set up a short working session to identify which buyer, admin, or operator role is still missing from the value loop, and confirm the next step to close that gap?

Let me know a time that works and who on your side should join.

Best regards
output.reason: Priority factors indicate a yellow health signal (health_yellow, contribution 15) alongside low seat penetration (low_seat_penetration, contribution 12), for a combined score of 27. The cs_platform health_score observed 2026-07-03 (corpus-b-live-1) and an activation due_date observed 2026-07-10 (corpus-b-live-1-cta-1) support proposing a working session to verify whether the adoption gap is trending toward a timeline slip and to identify which missing role is needed for a complete rollout.

org_context.boundary: Org context may shape language and play selection only; customer-specific claims still require request evidence.

gap_plays.customer_ask: review the overdue activation steps and agree on owners
gap_plays.play: review the overdue activation milestones and assign owners

gap_plays.customer_ask: map the missing users needed for a complete rollout
gap_plays.play: identify which buyer, admin, or operator role is missing from the value loop

gap_plays.customer_ask: pick the next workflow to activate and remove the blocker
gap_plays.play: choose one purchased capability and unblock the next adoption step

gap_plays.customer_ask: confirm the success metric we should use for this rollout
gap_plays.play: confirm the stated success objective and how the customer will measure it

gap_plays.customer_ask: review the blocker behind the red health signal
gap_plays.play: separate the operational activation blocker from broader account health

gap_plays.customer_ask: check whether the current adoption gap is putting launch timing at risk
gap_plays.play: verify whether the risk is trending toward a timeline slip

terminology.activation: the customer reaches the first stable week of data-backed operations
terminology.asset: a vehicle or tracked field unit
terminology.capability: a product workflow the customer purchased and can adopt
terminology.play: a recommended CSM motion, not a commitment or approval

value_props.name: Operational visibility
value_props.summary: Bring purchased assets online so the customer can see adoption gaps early.

value_props.name: Workflow adoption
value_props.summary: Move teams from purchased capability to repeatable day-to-day use.

value_props.name: Owner alignment
value_props.summary: Make the next operational owner and blocker explicit before the timeline slips.

voice_rules: Name the operational blocker before asking for time.
Use plain language; avoid hype, discounts, pressure, or escalation language.
Ask for a concrete working session with the right customer owner.

output.reason: This account is set for internal review. The priority score of 27 is driven by a yellow health signal (health_yellow, contribution 15) observed on 2026-07-03 per corpus-b-live-2, and by low seat penetration (low_seat_penetration, contribution 12). There is also an activation-related milestone with a due date of 2026-07-10 per corpus-b-live-2-cta-1. The yellow health signal suggests verifying whether the adoption gap is trending toward a timeline slip, and the low seat penetration suggests identifying which buyer, admin, or operator role is missing from the value loop.
